title: Blade
review:
First watch of this and the movie so much better and funnier than i expected. It definitely seems like it's supposed to come off corny and it gets that perfectly. Wesley Snipes himself is a really good Blade, the story was good bar the weird incest undertones, and Frost was a fine villain.

Last Watched: March 24th, 2025

title: X-Men
review:
Honestly super good, i thought every character on the surface level was shown what their motivations were and how they'll be. The Rogue and Wolverine dynamic was incredibly well done i thought, and very sweet. I feel like Charles Xavier's casting and performance was downright perfect, and same with Magneto. Very very good movie to introduce you into some of the X-Men (first watch).

Last Watched: March 25th, 2025

title: Blade 2
review:
The action is quite good and the villains definitely got more time to be fleshed out which is amazing, however it was a lot less corny and silly than the last which is why i liked the first. Still not bad.

Last Watched: March 26th, 2025

title: X2: X-Men United
review:
Really really good i thought, everything about the film was great. Good intro to Nightcrawler, Logan and Jean had some great moments. Erik and Charles are the winners every single time. Their chemistry is unbridled, and their acting is just phenomenal. Magneto himself is a great villain, extremely consistent with how he feels towards mutants and humans, willing to help when it benefits him and willing to change paths for his personal beliefs when he shouldn't. He is just a wonderful villain. The only thing missing to make this perfect is the emotional aspect, i feel like i didn't connect with some of the sad moments portrayed.

Last Watched: March 28th, 2025

title: Blade: Trinity
review:
The action is quite average and the film itself doesn't seem to really tell a story that progresses, it stays the same for ages until it's boss fight time, but Ryan Reynolds' jokes were awful and the comedy as a whole was nowhere near silly or corny as it should be for a film like this. It wasn't funny, or action packed. It was just there.

Last Watched: March 29th, 2025

title: X-Men: The Last Stand
review:
So much happened here and so much of it got reversed with the end credit scene and last scene from the movie 😭 if we were gonna be ballsy we gotta stick to that direction. The movie itself was fine, a lot of shocking moments and stuff that felt rushed like Magneto getting the Brotherhood together and almost everything with Jean. The action was decent, not that much fight scenes up until the final battle and even then not many people had a direct shine. Emotionally, this movie resonated well. Magneto stole the show yet again, he is perfectly flawed.

Last Watched: April 9th, 2025

title: Fantastic Four: Rise of the Silver Surfer
X-Men Origins: Wolverine
review:
As a stand alone movie it's actually quite nice, but knowing the story going in and what will happen just doesn't leave any suspense or doubt. The twists it had did genuinely shock me. I do like the overall concept though, it's really interesting to learn just how old Logan is and how he got to where he is at. I'm not sure if this Deadpool is the same Deadpool we see in those movies, but if it is i'm really interested to see how we get there. Emma Frost cameo was super cool. If i had a nickel for every movie that had a version of Deadpool, Wolverine, and Gambit...

Last Watched: April 28th, 2025

title: X-Men: First Class
review:
I was thoroughly shocked at how much i liked this. i am never really a big fan of prequels, mainly because it can make what we have already seen feel lesser or not add up. There are some moments like that in this, but the acting and story alone were just really good. The younger Charles and Erik ate the acting up. Their chemistry, delivery, etc were just amazing and extremely compelling. They are def gay lovers i will not listen to any other suggestions.

Last Watched: April 29th, 2025

title: The Wolverine
review:
The end credit scene got this an extra half star, but the story itself was super... random? i felt like. i enjoyed the bits showcasing Logan's PTSD and desire to finally die and rest. Those story beats and his mental health showcasing was really really good. The entire story with the Yakuza however was not good to me. i felt like it was super out of place, and did NOT tie into to that overall story of his mental health no matter how hard they tried to shoehorn it in. However i did enjoy Yukio, she was really fun.

Last Watched: May 2nd, 2025

title: X-Men: Days of Future Past
review:
This movie has blown me away. Visually it looks phenomenal, the scene with Quicksilver running around the kitchen, a football stadium being lifted, depicting a dark but believable future. i'm blown away by how good the story was, it made sense and was very emotionally compelling not knowing genuinely what would happen. Magneto, younger this time, stole the show again. His character is so beautifully flawed. The only thing that holds it back is a time travel movie, hate time travel and the concept, but for what it was this was a good time travel movie.

Last Watched: May 2nd, 2025

title: Deadpool
review:
Very very funny and amazing action scenes. i also think the relationship between Wade and Vanessa was very well done and actually made for some nice emotional moments. The villain was a bit meh and some of the comedy definitely undercut the serious moments, but that's kinda the point of the movie. Really cool opener too.

Last Watched: May 13th, 2025

title: X-Men: Apocalypse
review:
I'd have preferred if the follow up to Days of Future Past at least followed up. This showcases the mutant abilities in a really cool way, but the timeline that was already confusing enough got more confusing for me here, and the actual movie itself was quite boring so it didn't really help. The Quicksilver scene was super cool, and i am a big fan of how Magneto's powers were done, but outside of that i didn't like much of it.

Last Watched: May 19th, 2025

title: Logan
review:
What a beautiful movie. Old Logan and old Charles just trying to survive together and Logan doing his best to keep Charles there since he has always been the best of Logan. It just shows how much the Professor always meant to him. Laura was a great addition, and her and Logan's chemistry together was amazing. Being rated R definitely helped the fight scenes and acting, some of the gore actually reminded me of The Boys, and i wonder if they got ideas from this. The concept of Logan simply just getting too old instead of any cure like what was proposed in The Wolverine is amazing, and this is the perfect send off to Logan overall. The X grave because he is THE X-Man too. Masterful.

Last Watched: June 2nd, 2025

title: Deadpool 2
review:
I enjoyed the film a lot, honestly i think it's kind of funnier than the first. The scene with all of the X-Men for a full second had me cackling, and i thought the actual story element behind the film was pretty good. The action scenes and music was also remarkably fun.

Last Watched: October 7th, 2025

title: X-Men: Dark Phoenix
review:
This is one of the most boring movies i've seen. And it's unfortunately not funny bad, it's just boring bad so it feels more exhausting to watch. It's not really the actors' faults either, it's just how rushed the story was compared to the new cast of actors and how weirdly written it was. James McAvoy and Fassbender are just so good, perfect follows to Ian McKellen and Patrick Stewart, it's a shame what their movies gave them. Very unfortunate end to the X-Men film lineup and not really shocked it was the last.

Last Watched: December 3rd, 2025

title: The New Mutants
review:
Yikes this is worse than i thought it'd be. It doesn't feel genuine at all idk how to describe it. i don't think any of their chemistry was all too good and i think the writing and story were just incredibly generic. The characters weren't all that memorable probably besides Magik, and the doctor was super easy to see coming. The worst part is being bored. A bad movie can be fun if it's funny bad, this was just boring bad.

Last Watched: February 15th, 2026
